stemHtml: During a flight within the NAT High Level Airspace (HLA) and on a westbound OTS track, a passenger becomes ill and an immediate diversion is required. Regarding a diversion to the nearest suitable airport, how should the crew mitigate for the risk posed to other aircraft if they do not receive an ATC clearance for the diversion?

options.a: Maintain altitude and make a turn to cross any adjacent tracks at 90° to exit the track system and then turn to the diversion airport.
options.b: Continue on the assigned track and offset the altitude by 500 ft before making a turn towards the diversion airport.
options.c: Offset the flightpath by 5 NM prior to initiating a descent to below FL290 before turning towards the selected diversion airport.
options.d: Continue to the next airspace boundary when there is a change of ATC centre and request a re-clearance to leave the NAT HLA tracks.
